title: allamanda cathartica var. williamsii
intro: Allamanda cathartica var. williamsii, also known as the Golden Trumpet Vine, is a flowering plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce emesis or vomiting, hence its name 'cathartica', which is derived from the Greek word for purging. The health benefits of Allamanda cathartica var. williamsii include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and tonics for medicinal purposes.
